channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: Compare Cloud and On-Device AI Costs Without Inventing Energy Numbers
item.description: “On-device AI saves battery” and “cloud AI is more efficient” can both sound plausible. Neither is a measurement. The placement decision crosses at least four different budgets: user wait +...

item.title: Verify a Self-Hosted Installer Before Running It as Root
item.description: Downloading an installer and immediately executing it as root collapses three operational decisions into one command: Which artifact? -> Did these bytes arrive intact? -> Should this host execute...

item.title: Add Arrow-Key Shortcuts to a Confirmation Dialog Without Breaking Accessibility
item.description: Two buttons in a confirmation dialog look simple: Cancel and Confirm. Keyboard behavior makes the component a small state machine. A recent MonkeyCode change gives us a concrete example. Issue...

item.title: GPT-5.6 MCP: Testing Servers With Sol, Terra & Luna
item.description: 📖 TL;DR GPT-5.6 shipped July 9, 2026 in three tiers Sol (flagship), Terra (balanced), and Luna (cheapest) all tuned for agentic tool calling. All three share a 1M-token context window, 128K max...

item.title: Measure Documentation Coverage for AI Agents With This Scorecard
item.description: The argument that AI makes documentation obsolete confuses two different activities: producing prose and preserving operational knowledge. An agent can generate prose cheaply. It cannot recover a...

item.title: Build a Reconnecting SSE Task Stream With Node.js
item.description: A long-running AI task needs to report more than “loading.” It may be queued, editing, testing, waiting for input, retrying, or complete. For a learning project, Server-Sent Events (SSE) are a...

item.title: Turn a GitHub Issue Into an AI-Ready Bug Packet
item.description: I like short bug reports right up to the moment I ask a coding agent to fix one. “Markdown links are broken” leaves the agent to invent the environment, the click behavior, the expected route, and...

item.title: Merge Two Link Lists | LeetCode Practice #6
item.description: Merge Two Sorted Lists You are given the heads of two sorted linked lists list1 and list2. Merge the two lists into one sorted list. Both lists are sorted in non-decreasing order. Return the head...

item.title: One Go codebase, two deploy modes: a monolith that becomes microservices without a rewrite
item.description: Every new backend starts with the same fork in the road, and both paths hurt. Start with microservices and you pay for distributed systems on day one — network hops, eventual consistency, five...

item.title: I built a HEIC to JPG converter that never uploads your photos (WebAssembly + libheif)
item.description: The privacy problem with "online HEIC converters", and how running libheif in the browser fixes it. If you've ever helped a non-technical person get an iPhone photo to open on a Windows laptop,...

item.title: Already rich, already successful, why the last wave of tech winners is grinding again
item.description: They're rolling up their sleeves again, seemingly out of fear of missing AI's defining moment and, presumably, the irresistible allure of making even more money -- potentially a lot more.

item.title: Uber’s product chief on hotels, robotaxis, and why the company doesn’t want to be “everything for everyone”
item.description: Uber Chief Product Officer Sachin Kansal walks TechCrunch through the company's financial-services ambitions, its increasingly complicated relationship with Waymo, its new AV Labs data operation,...

item.title: Video-generation startup PixVerse raises $439M, valuation soars past $2B
item.description: With the cash, the company aims to expand its world model offering and reach customers across geographies.

item.title: Hermes agent maker Nous Research in talks for new funding at $1.5B valuation
item.description: The company is raising at least $75 million, led by Robot Ventures, with significant participation from USV and other prominent investors.

item.title: Microsoft tests Windows Search without all the ads and fluff
item.description: Microsoft is testing a cleaner version of the Windows 11 search menu that strips it of recommended content and ads. In a blog post on Monday, Microsoft announced that it's rolling out the...

item.title: OnePlus is reportedly bailing on the US
item.description: OnePlus and its parent company, Oppo, plan to announce in the coming days that OnePlus brand will be leaving the US and European markets, according to a machine translation of a WinFuture report....

item.title: Siri AI Is Becoming Apple’s Everything Tool
item.description: Apple’s revamped Siri is more than a voice assistant; it’s now the backbone of the iPhone user experience. You can try it now through the iOS 27 public beta.

item.title: A two-pack of DJI’s most capable wireless mics just got its first price cut
item.description: Smartphones these days have incredible cameras that are capable of taking smooth, sharp video, but the microphones are often lacking, to say the least. A wireless lavalier microphone can...

item.title: The US government warns that Russia state hackers are coming after your router
item.description: With residential proxies all the rage, CISA urges router users to be vigilant.

item.title: Satya Nadella has issued a shocking warning to companies using AI
item.description: Of all the debates raging about the potential downsides of AI, there is one worry causing the most hand-wringing among AI enthusiasts in Silicon Valley — that the giant AI labs that sell...

item.title: The Shokz OpenRun Pro are the cheapest they’ve been since January
item.description: Noise-canceling earbuds are great for flights and focusing, but they're not always ideal for outdoor workouts. The last-gen Shokz OpenRun Pro's open-ear design lets you enjoy music while staying...

item.title: The Pixel colors might rule this year
item.description: This year's Google Pixel 11 lineup might come in a bunch of funky colors. A series of now-deleted Amazon listings spotted by 9to5Google show what appear to be placeholders for Google's upcoming...

item.title: Apple’s public betas for iOS 27 and more are out now
item.description: Apple has just released public betas for iOS 27 and other major OS updates that are set to publicly launch this fall. The big new feature this year is Siri AI, the delayed AI-powered revamp to...

item.title: Siri AI makes the Apple Watch finally feel like a wrist computer
item.description: Siri has been on the Apple Watch since day one, though I'm usually hard-pressed to find people who actually make good use of it. It's kind of just… been there - mostly as a way to set timers when...

item.title: Siri AI is already changing how I use my iPhone
item.description: iOS 27 escaped the developer world today with the launch of the first public beta. I've been testing the new operating system since early June, looking for quirks and seeing if it can live up to...

item.title: The macOS 27 public beta is worth it just for the Liquid Glass tweaks
item.description: The macOS 27 Golden Gate public beta is here, and anyone with an M-series Mac now has easier access to test-drive Apple's latest changes - including a more subdued Liquid Glass aesthetic. That's...

item.title: The wildest allegations in Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI
item.description: Apple’s trade secrets lawsuit against OpenAI contains allegations that range from employees joking about unauthorized access to Apple’s systems to claims that job candidates were asked to bring...

item.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company,...

item.title: CrashStealer macOS Malware Uses Notarized Dropper to Pass Gatekeeper Checks
item.description: Cybersecurity researchers have flagged a new macOS information stealer called CrashStealer that's capable of harvesting sensitive data from compromised systems. Unlike other information stealers...

item.title: Sam Altman’s space data center trash talk is what most experts already believe
item.description: Responding to Musk accusing him of being a scammer, Altman said, "homeboy you're the one sellling [sic] public market investors on short-term space datacenters."
